$schema: Each evidence record describes a single source document. 'proves' lists what the record establishes; 'doesNotProve' is what it CANNOT establish (so future claims don't overstate).

ev-1775-nc-deed-boston-best.extract: Boston Best (and wife Catron Best) sells land to George Cathey. Witnesses: George Lamkin, Andrew Cathey.
ev-1775-nc-deed-boston-best.citation: Tryon County, NC deed, 4 Feb 1775 (proved July 1775); grantee George Cathey; witnesses George Lamkin, Andrew Cathey.
proves: Boston Best present in Tryon Co., NC by 4 Feb 1775
Wife's recorded name was Catron Best
Boston Best was active in land transactions by 1775
doesNotProve: Catron's full given name (likely Catherine variant — not certain)
Catron's maiden name
Their children

ev-1771-bostian-bess-deed-nc.extract: Boston Bess acquires a tract of land (from John Ryan/Wyatt; deed recorded in court).
ev-1771-bostian-bess-deed-nc.citation: Lincoln County, NC deed records, ~1771 (grantor: John Ryan/Wyatt; grantee: Boston Bess).
proves: Boston Bess present in Lincoln County, NC by ~1771
Boston Bess acquired land in Lincoln Co., NC — earliest confirmed appearance
doesNotProve: Relationship to any other Bess individual
Connection to Missouri Bess line
Whether this is the same Boston as Boston Best Sr. (d. 1809)

ev-1783-bostian-bess-land-nc.extract: Boston (Bostian) Bess — 185 acres (referenced in court land chain).
ev-1783-bostian-bess-land-nc.citation: Lincoln County, NC court land chain exhibit, 13 Oct 1783.
proves: Boston/Bostian Bess held 185 acres in Lincoln Co., NC by 1783
Bostian Bess present in Lincoln Co., NC land chain
doesNotProve: Relationship to Jacob Bess (elder) or any other Bess individual
Connection to Missouri Bess line
Whether this is the same Boston as Boston Best Sr. (d. 1809)

ev-1822-jacob-bess-elder-court.extract: Jacob Bess named as defendant in trespass case.
ev-1822-jacob-bess-elder-court.citation: North Carolina court record, 27 Mar 1822 (trespass case; book/page TBD).
proves: Jacob Bess (elder) was present and active in North Carolina by 27 Mar 1822
doesNotProve: Outcome of the case
Kinship to any other Bess individual
Connection to Missouri Jacob Bess Sr.

ev-1839-bostian-best-referee.extract: Bostian Best named as court referee.
ev-1839-bostian-best-referee.citation: North Carolina court record, ~1839 (Bostian Best as referee; book/page TBD).
proves: A Bostian Best was active in North Carolina ~1839 and served as court referee
doesNotProve: That this is the same individual as Bostian Bess (1783 land reference)
Kinship to Jacob Bess or any other Bess/Best individual

ev-1832-michael-best-nc.extract: Michael Best appears in estate/account entry ~1832.
proves: Michael Best present in North Carolina ~1832
doesNotProve: Kinship to any other Best/Bess individual
Whether this is the same Michael Best as the ~1793 Lincoln Co. estate account (ev-1793-michael-best-estate)

ev-1839-jacob-bess-linebarger-deed.extract: Additional deed from Jacob Bess to Jonas R. Linebarger (Book 37, p. 547).
ev-1839-jacob-bess-linebarger-deed.citation: Lincoln County, NC deed, 1839, Book 37, p. 547.
proves: A second conveyance from Jacob Bess (elder) to Jonas R. Linebarger occurred in 1839
Confirms ongoing association between Jacob Bess and Linebarger consistent with care agreement
doesNotProve: Kinship between Jacob Bess and Linebarger
Connection to Missouri Jacob Bess Sr.

ev-1831-michael-best-deed.extract: Michael Best sells 135-acre tract on Long Creek to James Ferguson for $300.
ev-1831-michael-best-deed.citation: Lincoln County, NC deed, 15 Oct 1831, recorded Nov 1836; location: Long Creek.
proves: Michael Best held and sold a 135-acre tract in Lincoln Co., NC in 1831
James Ferguson was the purchaser
doesNotProve: Kinship between Michael Best and any other Best/Bess individual
Connection to Missouri Bess line

ev-1832-peter-bess-fanny-deed.extract: Peter Bess transfers enslaved person (Emeline) to Fanny Bess.
ev-1832-peter-bess-fanny-deed.citation: Lincoln County, NC deed, 7 Feb 1832, proved July 1832.
proves: Peter Bess and Fanny Bess both present in Lincoln Co., NC 1832
Peter Bess made a direct property transfer to Fanny Bess
doesNotProve: Family relationship between Peter Bess and Fanny Bess (same surname + direct transfer is SUPPORTED only)
Connection to any other Bess individual

ev-1805-jacob-bess-elder-deed.extract: Deed from Jacob Bess to Peter Smith (referenced in court land chain).
ev-1805-jacob-bess-elder-deed.citation: Lincoln County, NC deed, 16 Jun 1805 (referenced in court land chain; book/page TBD).
proves: Jacob Bess held and conveyed land in Lincoln Co., NC by 1805
doesNotProve: Kinship to any other Bess individual
Whether this is the same Jacob as the 1831 deed or 1837 care agreement (probable but requires further confirmation)
Connection to Missouri Jacob Bess Sr.

ev-1831-jacob-bess-elder-deed.extract: 4-acre tract from Jacob Bess to William Bluford (not recorded at time; referenced in court).
ev-1831-jacob-bess-elder-deed.citation: Lincoln County, NC deed reference, 24/25 Feb 1831 (unrec.; cited in court proceedings).
proves: A Jacob Bess conveyed a 4-acre tract in Lincoln Co., NC in 1831
doesNotProve: Kinship to any other Bess individual
That this is the same Jacob as the 1805 deed (probable given same county; not certain)
Connection to Missouri Jacob Bess Sr.

ev-1837-jacob-bess-elder-care.extract: Jacob Bess transfers property to Jonas R. Linebarger in exchange for lifetime care; reserves limited personal property.
ev-1837-jacob-bess-elder-care.citation: Lincoln County, NC bill of sale / care contract, 21 Mar 1837; proved 1839.
proves: Jacob Bess (elder) was alive in 1837 and arranged for lifetime care — indicating advanced age
Property transferred to Jonas R. Linebarger in exchange for lifetime maintenance
Jacob Bess retained limited personal property
doesNotProve: Kinship between Jacob Bess and Jonas R. Linebarger
Jacob Bess's death date
Connection to Missouri Jacob Bess Sr.

ev-1793-michael-best-estate.extract: Estate of Michael Best … to Jacob Best … [lists goods/services including corn, iron, planks, etc.]
ev-1793-michael-best-estate.citation: Estate/account record, Michael Best, Lincoln County, NC, ~1793 (full book/page TBD).
proves: Michael Best had an estate being settled ~1793 in Lincoln Co., NC
Jacob Best is named in Michael Best's estate account
Both Michael Best and Jacob Best were present in Lincoln Co., NC by ~1793
doesNotProve: Any family relationship between Michael Best and Jacob Best (no parent/child, heir, or kin language)
Connection to the Missouri Bess line
That Michael Best is the same generation as Boston Best

ev-1795-boston-daniel-best-deed.extract: Boston Best conveys land to Daniel Best. Mentions Jacob Best & Martin Best as adjoining landholders.
ev-1795-boston-daniel-best-deed.citation: Lincoln County, NC deed, 31 Dec 1795 (extract per user transcription; book/page TBD).
proves: Boston Best and Daniel Best both present in Lincoln Co., NC, 1795
Jacob Best and Martin Best held adjoining land in Lincoln Co., NC, 1795
doesNotProve: Kinship between Boston, Daniel, Jacob, or Martin Best
Connection to the Missouri Bess line

ev-1783-boston-best-deed-nc.extract: Boston Best sells ~128 acres to Wiley Rudisill.
ev-1783-boston-best-deed-nc.citation: Lincoln County, NC deed, 15 Feb 1783 (April Term 1783); grantor: Boston Best; grantee: Wiley Rudisill.

doesNotProve: Kinship between Boston Best and any other named individual

ev-1788-boston-best-peter-deed-nc.extract: Boston Best transfers ~100 acres to Peter Best.

proves: Boston Best transferred land to Peter Best in Lincoln Co., NC, 1788
Both Boston Best and Peter Best were present in Lincoln Co., NC, 1788
doesNotProve: Family relationship between Boston and Peter Best — transfer is consistent with kinship but not explicit

ev-1795-boston-best-frederick-deed.extract: Boston Best transfers ~31 acres to Frederick Best. Witnesses: Barney Murphy, And Taylor.

Frederick Best present in Lincoln Co., NC by 1795
doesNotProve: Family relationship between Boston and Frederick Best — transfer is consistent with kinship but not explicit
Whether Frederick Best is related to Daniel, Jacob, or Martin Best

ev-1782-peter-best-deed-nc.extract: Peter Best buys land from Martin Hoyle.

proves: Peter Best present in Lincoln Co., NC by 12 Mar 1782 and acquiring land
doesNotProve: Relationship to Boston Best
Connection to Missouri Bess line

ev-1788-peter-best-deed-nc.extract: Peter Best's land in chain of title described as originating from Boston Best.
ev-1788-peter-best-deed-nc.citation: Lincoln County, NC deed, 9 Oct 1788; grantee: Peter Best; land origin described as from Boston Best.
proves: Peter Best held land in Lincoln Co., NC whose chain of title traced to Boston Best by 1788
doesNotProve: Explicit family relationship between Boston and Peter Best — chain-of-title connection is consistent with kinship or direct purchase

ev-1792-peter-best-deed-nc.extract: Peter Best purchases land; deed references Boston Best line.

Relationship of Andrew or Jonah to Peter beyond their named roles

doesNotProve: Any family relationship between Jacob Best and Peter Bess

ev-1847-hiram-bess-will.extract: Will of Hiram Bess; wife Eliza; son John Franklin; daughters Mary Ann, Jane, Catharine, Margaret Elizabeth, Susan Adoline. Prohibits sale of enslaved persons by Eliza or any future husband. Authorizes Eliza to liquidate property as needed to satisfy estate debts. Appoints Eliza Bess and John F. Hill as co-executors. Witnessed by Thomas Berry Spratt, Peter Grigg, and Lawson Bess. Will proven; executors sworn.
ev-1847-hiram-bess-will.citation: 5 Nov 1847 will of Hiram Bess, Lincoln County, NC.
proves: Hiram Bess died by Nov 1847 in Lincoln Co., NC
Wife Eliza Bess named and appointed co-executor
Children named: John Franklin, Mary Ann, Jane, Catharine, Margaret Elizabeth, Susan Adoline
John F. Hill appointed co-executor (role only)
Witnesses present: Thomas Berry Spratt, Peter Grigg, Lawson Bess (roles proven)
Will formally admitted to probate; executors sworn
doesNotProve: Family relationship of John F. Hill to Hiram Bess
Family relationship of witnesses (Spratt, Grigg, Lawson Bess) to Hiram Bess
That Lawson Bess (witness) is a child of Hiram or related to MO Lawson Bess
Connection to Missouri Bess line

ev-1816-peter-critz-guardian-settlement.extract: Peter Critz guardian of David & Jacob Slinkard (minors); settlement.
proves: Two minor Slinkards (David, Jacob) had Peter Critz as guardian as of 1816
doesNotProve: Their father's identity (this record alone)
Connection to main Slinkard line
ev-1816-peter-critz-guardian-settlement.notes: May be the same proceeding as ev-jacob-slinkard-mo-minors-guardianship — pending user verification.

ev-jacob-slinkard-mo-minors-guardianship.extract: Guardianship/court record naming Jacob Slinkard (deceased) and his minor children: Polly Slinkard, Barbara Slinkard, David Slinkard, Jacob Slinkard.
ev-jacob-slinkard-mo-minors-guardianship.citation: User-provided summary; original record citation TBD.
proves: Polly, Barbara, David, and Jacob Slinkard are all children of a Jacob Slinkard who was deceased at the time of the proceeding
doesNotProve: Jacob Slinkard's exact death date
Whether this 'deceased Jacob Slinkard' is the same individual as the Jacob Slinkard who sold a town lot in 1818 — see conflict note
Maiden name of mother (mother not named here)
ev-jacob-slinkard-mo-minors-guardianship.notes: TEMPORAL CONFLICT WITH ev-1818-jacob-slinkard-mo-deed. The 1818 deed has Jacob alive. If the date range ~1813–1816 is correct AND this is the same Jacob, the 1818 deed cannot also refer to him. Possibilities: (a) date range is actually later (e.g. ~1819–1822); (b) two different Jacob Slinkards in MO; (c) 1818 deed refers to a different Jacob. See review/conflicts.md and task t-jacob-slinkard-guardianship-date.

ev-1850-census-john-bess-madison.extract: John Bess, age 59, born North Carolina; Catherine Bess, age 59, born North Carolina; household also includes Maliny (24), Emanuel (22), Absolum (19), Rua (17), Pernicia (14) — all born Missouri.
proves: John Bess present in Madison Co., MO 1850
Born ~1791, birthplace North Carolina
Catherine Bess same age, same birthplace, in same household
Five individuals (Maliny, Emanuel, Absolum, Rua, Pernicia) born Missouri present in household
doesNotProve: Catherine is John's wife (household co-residence only; 1850 census did not record relationships)
Listed individuals are John's children (household only)

ev-1850-jacob-bess-cluster-analysis.extract: Jacob Bess Sr. fits chronologically (~1798) as second-generation son of Peter Best (b. ~1765–1775); migration timing aligns with NC→MO ~1815–1822; no conflicting candidate.
ev-1850-jacob-bess-cluster-analysis.citation: Working analysis combining the above sources.
doesNotProve: Direct parent-child relationship between Peter Best and Jacob Bess Sr.
ev-1850-jacob-bess-cluster-analysis.notes: Analytical record only; supports SUPPORTED status, never PROVEN.
